description: Compare the drug interaction profiles of two prostate cancer treatments, 'Enzalutamide' and 'Abiraterone', for a patient currently taking 'Apixaban' (Anticoagulant) and 'Simvastatin' (Lipid Lowering Agent). Document the interaction traffic light colors for all four drug combinations and identify which prostate cancer treatment is the safer choice for this patient. Save the report to /sdcard/mcrpc_therapy_choice.txt.

metadata.clinical_reasoning: Enzalutamide is a strong CYP3A4 inducer which significantly reduces Apixaban levels (stroke risk). Abiraterone has a more favorable profile.
